title: As Chinese Tech Pulls Ahead, U.S. Fears It Will Become Dependent
summary: Chinese firms have some of the world’s most advanced technology. But U.S. officials say relying on it could come with a downside.

title: China Takes Supercomputer Crown From U.S. For First Time Since 2017
summary: A supercomputer in Shenzhen was declared the world’s fastest. It uses only standard microprocessors and not the special-purpose chips called graphics processing units.

title: The Iran War’s Persistent Threat to Farmers in Poor Countries
summary: A reopening of the Strait of Hormuz would do little to swiftly ease the pain inflicted by higher prices for fertilizer, food and fuel in Ivory Coast.

title: Supreme Court Bars Rastafarian Man From Suing Prison Guards for Shaving His Dreadlocks
summary: Damon Landor, a Rastafarian, tried to sue Louisiana prison officials for violating his religious rights.

title: Supreme Court Rejects Lawsuit Claiming Cisco Systems Helped China Target Falun Gong
summary: The court’s decision could have broader implications for whether companies can be held liable for aiding in international human rights abuses.

title: Supreme Court Sides With Exxon in Lawsuit Over Assets Seized by Cuba
summary: The Trump administration backed Exxon Mobil’s effort to be compensated for oil and gas assets confiscated by the Cuban government in 1960.

title: Britain Is Still Deep in the Shadow of Brexit
summary: Ten years after a slim majority voted to leave the European Union, the economic and political effects of that decision continue to disrupt the United Kingdom.

title: Brexit Has Cost the UK Growth, Analysts Say, in the Decade Since the Vote
summary: Citing lower trade and investment, analysts broadly agree that Britain’s economy is smaller than it would have been if the country had stayed in the E.U.

title: The Extraordinary Embezzlement Case That Rocked Scottish Politics
summary: Peter Murrell, the husband of the former Scottish leader Nicola Sturgeon, was sentenced on Tuesday after he admitted to buying a bizarre range of items with the Scottish National Party’s money.

title: Searching for Clues in Jeffrey Epstein’s Boyhood
summary: “Jeff” grew up in an insular world, kept company with a few brainy boys and fixated on girls. Was a monster hiding in plain sight?

title: How Trump Turned America’s Refugee Program Into a Pathway for White People
summary: President Trump has created an exception to his refugee ban for white South Africans, reshaping a program intended for people fleeing persecution and disaster.

title: A Look Inside the Welcome Bags Planned for White South African Refugees
summary: The United States is putting together bags with a children’s book on so-called reverse racism, and with a document that defends the country’s founding on the basis of slavery.

title: Goldman Says Cafe’s Hostile Post About Him Does Not Warrant Civil Rights Case
summary: After a Justice Department official said her office was opening an investigation, Representative Dan Goldman said the department should focus on “other people in the Jewish community who are really scared.”

title: Meta Has Created a Prediction Markets App
summary: The experimental app, internally called “Arena,” would be independent of Facebook and Instagram. It could compete for attention with Polymarket and Kalshi, the biggest prediction markets.

title: Markets Recoil in Global Sell-Off Driven by Tech Stocks
summary: Chipmakers led the way down in South Korea, where the main index plunged 10.5 percent. The Nasdaq in the U.S. was down 2.2 percent at Tuesday’s open.

title: SpaceX Stock Price Is Slumping After Its Blockbuster IPO
summary: On Tuesday, SpaceX briefly fell below $150 a share, which was where the stock began trading when the company listed publicly just over a week ago.

title: Photos Behind Kennedy Center Tarps Show No Sign of Trump’s Name
summary: Images circulated by an activist group reveal bare marble where President Trump’s name once resided. The Kennedy Center previously told a federal judge it had been removed.

title: Protesters Accused of Antifa Ties Sentenced to Up to 100 Years in ICE Attack
summary: The penalties, issued in an attack where a police officer was shot, dwarfed those given to Jan. 6 rioters and appeared to signal that at least some courts will deal aggressively with ICE protesters.

title: Letter From Conservatives Prompted Inquiry of Civil Rights Group, Lawyers Say
summary: Several right-wing groups sent a letter in September to Stephen Miller, a top aide to President Trump, urging greater scrutiny of the Southern Poverty Law Center.

title: Looking Back on the U.S.A.’s Many Founders, 250 Years In
summary: In the past 50 years, the way we tell the story of the Revolution has become dramatically more complex. Can it still inspire us all?

title: Europe Is Sweltering in an Extreme Heat Wave. Here’s the Latest.
summary: France’s average temperature on Tuesday was its hottest ever, the country’s weather agency said. Several countries are under top-level heat warnings.

title: 40 People Drown in France Amid Scorching Temperatures
summary: Most of the victims were young people swimming in unsupervised areas, officials said.

title: Europe’s Heat Has Scientists Asking: How Much Hotter Can It Get?
summary: Records are being broken for the second time in a month, leading scientists to probe the upper limits of what the warming climate can dish out.

title: Social Security Is Running Out of Money, Fast
summary: To save the program, we need to eliminate the payroll tax cap.

title: Is This the Year Florida Turns Blue?
summary: David Jolly must persuade voters who have been increasingly hostile to Democrats that he’s a different kind of Democrat.

title: Ebola Symptoms in Current Outbreak May Be Milder Than in Previous Ones
summary: That is good news for patients, but officials fear it will make controlling the spread of the disease harder.
